title: BlazEr1 X-ray-anchored TeV-favourable population branch: reproducibility and external-cohort transfer package
description:
Reproducibility package for the manuscript An X-ray-anchored TeV-favourable population branch in eROSITA blazars.

The release preserves the frozen 5865-source BlazEr1 analysis, exact public catalogue snapshots, active-score and follow-up catalogues, source code, statistical audits, figure source data, vector figures, and provenance metadata. It also contains a frozen external-cohort transfer audit based on 2346 non-overlapping 4LAC-DR3 sources and 57 firm extragalactic TeVCat associations. The X-ray/IR-independent frozen projection reaches AUROC 0.901 (95% stratified-bootstrap interval 0.863–0.935); the 944-source complete-case result is 0.888 (0.840–0.930).

The scores are empirical population coordinates and are not calibrated TeV detection probabilities. The 4LAC analysis is a transportability audit, not evidence that the X-ray hierarchy is universal across catalogue selections.
notes: Original code is additionally licensed under MIT. Derived tables, figures, and original documentation are CC BY 4.0. Frozen third-party catalogue files retain their original archive terms and are not relicensed. No specific funding was received.
